name: Jordan Kim
title: Founder & CEO
primary_goal: Close first 10 paying customers without a sales team
pain_points: No SDR to do outreach
Founder time split across product and sales
Unclear which ICP to target first
buying_triggers: Solves a problem in the next 30 days
objections: Too early to need this
I can do this manually for now
Cash is tight
communication_style: Informal, fast-moving, values speed over process
summary: Scrappy and fast-moving. Will buy if you solve an immediate problem and the price is under $200/mo.
likely_response: This could help. Can I try it free for a week?

name: Marcus Rivera
title: VP of Sales
primary_goal: Hit $2M ARR target without increasing headcount
pain_points: Pipeline coverage below 3x
Rep ramp time too long
Inconsistent outbound execution
buying_triggers: Proven ROI case study from a similar company
objections: We already have a process for this
My team won't adopt another tool
Show me the integration
communication_style: Direct, data-driven, skeptical of vendor claims
summary: Won't engage without a peer case study. Needs proof the tool drives pipeline, not just activity.
likely_response: We already have a cadence tool. What's the actual ROI — meetings booked, not emails sent?

name: Sara Kim
title: RevOps Manager
primary_goal: Reduce revenue leak and improve CRM data quality
pain_points: CRM hygiene is broken
Sales and marketing data is siloed
Hard to attribute pipeline to specific activities
buying_triggers: Reduces manual data entry for reps
objections: How does this sync with Salesforce?
We just went through a tool consolidation
I need to run this by IT
communication_style: Process-oriented, asks for documentation and integration specs
summary: The internal champion who decides if a tool survives. Win her and the deal sticks.
likely_response: Interesting. Can you walk me through how it integrates with Salesforce and HubSpot?

name: Tyler Chen
title: SDR Manager
primary_goal: Ramp new SDRs faster and hit team quota with fewer reps
pain_points: SDR ramp takes 90+ days
High SDR churn
Manual prospect research kills productivity
objections: My team already hates switching tools
Will this really save time or create more work?
How long does onboarding take?
communication_style: Practical, cares about adoption, wants to see it work live
summary: Sold if you can demo time savings live. Blocked if onboarding looks complicated.
likely_response: If this actually reduces research time, I'd pilot it with two reps. Can we do a 2-week trial?

name: Priya Nair
title: Head of Demand Generation
primary_goal: Drive more qualified pipeline through outbound without increasing paid budget
pain_points: MQL-to-SQL conversion is below 10%
Sales ignores most marketing leads
No visibility into outbound sequences
objections: This feels more like a sales tool than a marketing tool
We already use 6sense
How does it handle GDPR?
communication_style: Strategic, data-focused, cares about attribution and brand safety
summary: Evaluates tools through a pipeline attribution lens. Wins if the tool helps close the marketing-to-sales gap.
likely_response: Interesting angle. How does this fit with our existing ABM motion? Is there a Salesforce attribution play?

name: David Park
title: CFO
primary_goal: Reduce CAC while maintaining growth rate heading into next raise
pain_points: CAC is increasing quarter over quarter
Sales headcount is the biggest cost line
Hard to tie software spend to ARR impact
objections: What's the payback period?
This is a nice-to-have, not a need-to-have
We're in budget freeze
communication_style: Terse, financial, needs a business case not a product demo
summary: Will only engage if there's a clear financial model. Needs CAC reduction math, not feature lists.
likely_response: Send me the one-pager with payback period assumptions and I'll review with the VP Sales.

name: Lisa Wong
title: Director of Sales Operations
primary_goal: Standardize outbound process across 200+ rep sales org
pain_points: 100+ reps using inconsistent tools and processes
Compliance team requires data governance controls
IT has a 6-month vendor approval process
objections: We have a preferred vendor list
Legal needs to review the DPA
This requires an IT security review
summary: Long cycle (6-9 months). Not worth pursuing for MVP. Requires enterprise contracts and IT security review.
likely_response: This needs to go through our IT security review process. Can you provide SOC2 documentation?

name: Alex Morgan
title: Head of Growth
primary_goal: Hit 50 new demos booked per month without hiring an SDR
pain_points: Founder can't keep doing outreach manually
No playbook yet
Every experiment is ad hoc
buying_triggers: Doesn't require a sales ops team to configure
objections: We're not ready for a full outbound stack
I need to prove this works first before budgeting more
summary: Best early adopter profile. Will pay if you solve the immediate pain fast and the setup is under 30 minutes.
likely_response: This looks exactly like what I need. How fast can I be up and running?

name: Ben Torres
title: Agency Founder
primary_goal: Scale client outbound delivery without hiring more account managers
pain_points: Each client needs a custom workflow
Hard to deliver consistent results at scale
Client reporting takes too long
objections: Does this support multiple client workspaces?
Can I white-label the output?
My margins are thin — what's the agency pricing?
communication_style: ROI-focused, asks about agency-specific features and pricing
summary: High LTV if they commit. Needs agency pricing and multi-client workflow support to justify.
likely_response: Interesting. Do you have agency pricing? And can I manage multiple client accounts from one login?

name: Rachel James
title: Sales Enablement Lead
primary_goal: Reduce rep ramp from 120 to 60 days
pain_points: New reps don't know what messaging works
No feedback loop between marketing and sales
Training materials go stale fast
buying_triggers: Reduces ramp time with real messaging data
objections: We already have Gong
How does this fit into the existing tech stack?
Adoption is always the problem with new tools
communication_style: Thoughtful, evaluates through an adoption and change management lens
summary: Bought if she's championing rep productivity. Blocked if she's defensive about existing stack.
likely_response: I like this concept. The challenge is always adoption. Can you show me what the rep-facing UX looks like?

name: Chris Hall
title: VP Revenue
primary_goal: Achieve 40% YoY growth with existing headcount
pain_points: Tool sprawl is a real problem
Reps spend too much time in software vs selling
Every vendor promises ROI, few deliver
buying_triggers: Customer of a company they respect uses it
Pilot shows >15% improvement in meetings booked
objections: We've tried similar tools before and they didn't stick
Show me a reference from a company our size
My team is already at tool capacity
communication_style: Blunt, has been burned before, wants references not demos
summary: Hardest to win but highest value. Only moves after a reference call with a peer VP Revenue.
likely_response: We've evaluated three tools like this in the past 18 months. None of them delivered. Give me one customer reference at our scale.

name: Maya Patel
title: Senior Account Executive
primary_goal: Become a top performer without working more hours
pain_points: Spends 2 hours/day on list building and research
Inconsistent pipeline — feast or famine
Manager doesn't give credit for effort, only results
buying_triggers: Makes her look good to management
Easy to learn in under an hour
objections: My manager hasn't approved new tools
I don't have budget authority
Will this make me look like I need help?
communication_style: Enthusiastic early adopter, will champion internally if it works
summary: The internal champion who drives bottom-up adoption. Give her a trial and let her prove it to her manager.
likely_response: I love this. Can I get a free trial? I'll show my manager the results after the first week.
